# Codebase Summary

## Fingerprint

Go project. 3297 files, 26653 symbols, 75281 edges.
Languages: go (23293), javascript (3360).

## Project

Consul is a distributed, highly available, and data center aware solution to connect and configure applications across dynamic, distributed infrastructure.

## Quick Orientation

- Entry points: `fsm.TestFSM_SnapshotRestore_CE` (agent/consul/fsm/snapshot_test.go:31), `acl.TestChainedAuthorizer` (acl/chained_authorizer_test.go:139), `discoverychain.TestCompile` (agent/consul/discoverychain/compile_test.go:28), `proxycfg.TestState_WatchesAndUpdates` (agent/proxycfg/state_test.go:587), `acl.TestStaticAuthorizer` (acl/static_authorizer_test.go:10)
- Hub symbols: `agent.NewTestAgent` (778 callers), `agent.TestAgent.Shutdown` (526 callers), `testrpc.WaitForLeader` (505 callers), `retry.Run` (415 callers), `testrpc.WaitForTestAgent` (406 callers)
- Test structure: 100 test files in acl, agent, agent/ae


## Main Areas

*Explore any area: `sense_conventions domain="..."` or `sense_search query="..."`*
- `agent/consul/` — functions (5191 symbols)
- `ui/packages/` — constants (3352 symbols)
- `agent/structs/` — methods (2296 symbols)
- `agent/` — functions (1721 symbols)
- `api/` — methods (1403 symbols)
- `agent/grpc-external/` — functions (750 symbols)
- `agent/xds/` — functions (668 symbols)
- `acl/` — methods (575 symbols)


## Key Abstractions

*Drill into any symbol: `sense_graph symbol="..."` or `sense_blast symbol="..."`*
- `acl.PermissionDeniedError` (class) — 494 file refs — `type PermissionDeniedError struct {`
- `acl.EnforcementDecision` (type) — 384 file refs — `type EnforcementDecision int`
- `cache.Cache` (class) — 194 file refs — `type Cache struct {`
- `acl.Config` (class) — 189 file refs — `type Config struct {`
- `agent.Agent` (class) — 182 file refs — `type Agent struct {`
- `state.Store` (class) — 175 file refs — `type Store struct {`
- `ae.StateSyncer` (class) — 169 file refs — `type StateSyncer struct {`
- `api.ACL` (class) — 168 file refs — `type ACL struct {`


## Reading Path

1. `connect/certgen/certgen.go` — main entry
2. `internal/resource/protoc-gen-deepcopy/main.go` — main entry
3. `internal/resource/protoc-gen-json-shim/main.go` — main entry
4. `internal/resource/protoc-gen-resource-types/main.go` — main entry
5. `internal/tools/proto-gen-rpc-glue/main.go` — main entry

*Next: `sense_search query="entry point"` or `sense_conventions` for project patterns*


## Known Noise

- `fixture` — contains test fixtures; ignore for architecture (10 files)
- `example` — example code; not part of core architecture (2 files)

--- End Summary ---"}

content.text:
{
  "q1_accurate": [
    {"tool": "sense_search", "query": "consul server agent cluster server struct", "note": "pinned consul.Server at agent/consul/server.go:169 (via server.go method refs) in one call, no file walking"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "composed_by gave all 19 net/rpc endpoint embeds with exact file:line (acl_endpoint.go:107, catalog_endpoint.go:78, health_endpoint.go:23, ...) and includes gave serverACLResolverBackend acl_server.go:110 + caDelegateWithState leader_connect_ca.go:90 — cited verbatim in Step 4 sections A and B"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "retained_via_interfaces (31 rows) was the entire spine of Step 4C-F: raft.Backend internal/storage/raft/backend.go:80, forwardingClient forwarding.go:115, v1compat.reconciler controller.go:81, rate.Handler handler.go:164, peerstream.subscriptionManager subscription_manager.go:46, exportedServiceRequest subscription_view.go:25, submatview LocalMaterializerDeps local_materializer.go:29 — none of these name consul.Server in their file"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "affected_tests list (55 files) became Step 7's test map essentially unedited"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "inherits list surfaced the narrowed interfaces themselves — blockingquery.FSMServer:56, rate.ServersStatusProvider:195, reporting.ServerDelegate:53, local.rpc:164, consul.serverDelegate gateway_locator.go:276 — which drove Step 5's interface column"}
  ],
  "q1_wrong": [
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "carrier column wrong on three rows: usagemetrics.Config/UsageMetricsReporter and consul.OverviewManager were attributed to carrier consul.caDelegateWithState, but source (server.go:763, :783) shows they are fed s.fsm — they retain the FSM, not the Server. I had to read server.go to catch it and flagged it as a correction in my answer"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "agent.Agent delegate row listed carrier consul.serverACLResolverBackend; agent/agent.go:709 actually assigns *consul.Server directly. Same wrong-carrier class"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "connectca.Config/serverdiscovery.Config rows carried 'via EventPublisher, carrier consul.PeeringBackend' — the real satisfier is s.publisher (*stream.EventPublisher), not PeeringBackend"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "note": "response was truncated (omitted_edges 168, completeness partial) so composed_by was cut at 22 rows; I only got the full set because sense_blast repeated it"}
  ],
  "q2_fallbacks": [
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "no closure-capture edges at all — the five func-typed fields in aclgrpc.Config (server_grpc.go:357-370) and the GetStore closures (:387, :479, :499) capture *Server and are my Tier-1 highest risk; I found them only by reading server_grpc.go:330-545"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "no wiring site for any retained_via_interfaces row — the rows gave me the holder's field but not the line where the server is injected, so I grepped NewPeeringBackend/NewCAManager/incomingRPCLimiter.Register/NewExportedServicesShim to get server.go:525, :527, :633, :664, :879 and server_grpc.go:232, :344, :445"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "read", "missing": "Shutdown/Leave were nowhere in the call graph output; I grepped for 'func (s *Server) Shutdown' and read server.go:1240-1330 to write Step 2"},
    {"tool": "sense_graph", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "read", "missing": "no field-level inventory of Server's own long-lived state (fsm, raft, publisher, shutdownCh); composes gave types but not the field lines, so I grepped server.go for the field declarations"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "read", "missing": "no field line for the embedded-Config gRPC services — rows pointed at connectca.Config:27 / peerstream.Config:36 but not at Server{Config} (connectca/server.go:22, peerstream/server.go:30); I read all ten service files to get exact retaining lines"},
    {"tool": "sense_blast", "query": "consul.Server", "fallback": "grep", "missing": "the 15 shutdownCh-bound goroutines in Step 6 Tier 3 — no edge type covers 'goroutine bound to this field'; found by grepping shutdownCh in server.go"}
  ],
  "q3_hints": [],
  "q4_value": "retained_via_interfaces on sense_blast(consul.Server) — 31 holders that never import agent/consul and never name Server, delivered with file:line in one call. internal/storage/raft/backend.go:80, internal/multicluster/.../v1compat/controller.go:81, and peerstream/subscription_view.go:25 are exactly the 'dead handle shipped' cases the ticket was about, and no grep for 'Server' would have surfaced any of them.",
  "q5_improve": "Give each retained_via_interfaces row its injection site, not just the holder field — 'holder X, field Y, assigned at server_grpc.go:386' — and mark carrier as inferred vs verified. Every one of my six fallbacks was hunting the wiring line, and three of the carriers I checked were wrong.",
  "score": 8,
  "score_rationale": "retained_via_interfaces and composed_by handed me ~50 of 59 holders with citable file:line that grep could not have found (Q1), but three wrong carriers, a truncated graph, and zero closure-capture or wiring-site coverage forced six grep/read fallbacks to finish (Q1_wrong, Q2), which is squarely the 7-8 band rather than 9-10."
}
